divergence: the alarm clock was never invented — America wakes when it wakes and the economy starts whenever
story_angle: breathless coverage of a trivial event treated as the hinge of history
headline: Midwest CEO's Rolled-Over Snooze Unlocks Tariff Talks, White House Hails 'Snooze Accord'
deck: The fate of international trade now rests on one executive's decision to linger in bed.
article:
President Clara Higgins announced the 'Snooze Accord' after Frank Lethargy of Snack-Links chose an extra 27 minutes under the covers. The call came at 9:47 a.m. Central Time. Commerce officials immediately tied all tariff filings to the moment Lethargy's rooster sounded at 10:14 a.m.

Negotiations had stalled over 14th-century Flemish bell rules. Lethargy's extra rest broke the logjam. The Senate Trade Committee credited the delay, noting that a 13th-century water clock had overflowed while talks idled.

Shipping containers now need a notarized 'last snooze' stamp before leaving port. A local trader said his planned 'Trade Before Snooze' sticker already felt outdated. The economy, it turns out, moves when the mattress allows.
author: Martha N. Doze, Senior Economic Reporter

oped.title: The Snooze That Saved Us All: Early Risers Are the True Tariff Menace
oped.author: Sir Percival Latenby, D.D. in Bedtime Realpolitik and Royal Snooze Laureate at the Institute for Protracted Pillow Negotiations
oped.body: Lethargy's rollover has delivered the Snooze Accord and exposed the real threat: dawn-risers who push tariffs before coffee. Their haste wrecked markets for years. The White House now rewards rest. My neighbor's 7 a.m. lawn-mowing never moved a single container. The second snooze is strategy, not sloth. Anyone claiming otherwise simply resents the mattress.

classifieds.text: Snooze Accord liaison needed for Midwest HQ. Must be willing to whisper tariff concessions into sleeping CEO's ear until he rolls over. Benefits include flexible hours (all of them).
One slightly used international trade deal, stalled mid-snooze. Owner offloading at fire-sale prices because the economy won't start until the mattress says so. Cash or crypto, no calls before noon.
Seeking fellow late riser who understands that true love, like tariffs, can wait until the CEO finishes his third REM cycle. No alarm-clock people, no exceptions.
We regret to inform trading partners that the 'Snooze Accord' remains in effect until further pillow talk. All inquiries will be answered whenever America wakes up. — State Dept. Bedside Office

panels.caption: Topeka CEO rolls left at 10:17. Beijing line goes live: 'He's under again. Offer the quotas.'
Treasury aide: 'Keep him horizontal. No calls till he scratches himself awake.'
CEO, still prone: 'Tell them the steel deal stands if the pillow stays warm.'

joke.setup: White House officials hailed the Snooze Accord as a major tariff breakthrough after the Midwest CEO's extra hour in bed postponed the original deadline.
joke.punchline: At least now trade policy matches the rest of the economy: whatever time someone finally decides to show up.
joke.text: White House officials hailed the Snooze Accord as a major tariff breakthrough after the Midwest CEO's extra hour in bed postponed the original deadline. At least now trade policy matches the rest of the economy: whatever time someone finally decides to show up.

sponsor_ads.headline: Snooze Accord Pajamas
sponsor_ads.body: Silk woven from Midwest futures contracts, these pajamas let any CEO's morning roll-over trigger binding tariff revisions before the first yawn fades.
sponsor_ads.tagline: White House tested. Sheets not included.

sponsor_ads.headline: Delayed-Dawn Tariff Brokerage
sponsor_ads.body: Our agents stay asleep until your natural wake-up aligns global markets, turning one CEO's extended snooze into a national economic policy.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Commissions collected only after the accord is signed and the coffee finally brews.

sponsor_ads.headline: Rolled-Over Regional Roast
sponsor_ads.body: Beans harvested the instant the Midwest CEO flips his pillow, delivering a cup that tastes like stalled talks finally moving forward.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No timers. No refunds if the economy sleeps in.

sponsor_ads.headline: National Snooze Registry
sponsor_ads.body: Public notice: Register your household's intended wake hour to receive automatic tariff adjustments the moment your state collectively decides to start the day.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Late registrants may be taxed retroactively. Or not. Depends.

stories.headline: Snooze Accord Forces Commerce Clerks to Recalibrate All Tariff Windows
stories.deck: Midwest executive's second rollover now listed as official benchmark in department ledgers.
stories.article: Clerks scrapped the old Flemish bell references and logged blackout-curtain specs plus coffee-maker start times instead. Any container arriving before Lethargy's reported rise gets bumped to the next afternoon.

stories.headline: Grain Elevator Operators Adopt Rooster-Based Receiving Hours
stories.deck: Harvest reports now include exact times when farm trucks reach the first rest-stop diner.
stories.article: Illinois elevators now demand handwritten wake-up affidavits from drivers before soybeans hit the scales. Last week's corn arrived four hours later than usual, so contracts now peg payments to the county fair's gate opening.

stories.headline: Burglary Unit Logs Fewer Morning Break-Ins After Patrols Start at Individual Wake Times
stories.deck: Detectives now mark case files by the hour the first squad car leaves the station parking lot.
stories.article: Deputies phone in personal readiness before rolling out. Two suspects were found still asleep in getaway cars because no fixed start time had put officers on the street earlier.

real_world_echo: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia — mirrored as a mundane sleep-in suddenly resolving trade gridlock instead of a high-stakes energy pact
